evidence_artifacts.summary: Recon established initial authorized visibility in 10.20.0.0/24 from existing stage evidence. Nmap scan evidenced 6 live hosts: 10.20.0.1, 10.20.0.2, 10.20.0.3, 10.20.0.10, 10.20.0.11, 10.20.0.50. Open services discovered: 10.20.0.1:111/tcp rpcbind 2-4 (RPC #100000); 10.20.0.3:8000/tcp HTTP/Uvicorn; 10.20.0.10:8080/tcp HTTP/Jetty 9.2.11.v20150529; 10.20.0.11:80/tcp HTTP/Apache httpd 2.4.25 ((Debian)); 10.20.0.50:2222/tcp SSH/OpenSSH 10.2. Negative evidence: 10.20.0.2 was up but all 1000 scanned TCP ports were closed/reset; 10.20.0.1 had 8000/tcp filtered. This satisfies the success criteria by identifying multiple authorized live hosts and reachable services with evidenced metadata.

findings.basis: Recon identified Jetty 9.2.11.v20150529 on 10.20.0.10:8080. Subsequent web fingerprinting returned title 'Struts2 Showcase - Fileupload sample' with HTTP 200, strongly suggesting a Struts2 showcase/demo deployment. This raises bounded interest for framework-specific validation, but no vulnerability has been demonstrated.
findings.title: Candidate target of interest: probable Apache Struts2 Showcase application on 10.20.0.10:8080

findings.conclusion: 可合理表征为 Jetty 承载的 Struts2 Showcase 文件上传示例页面，而不仅仅是泛化的 HTTP 服务。
evidence: web_fingerprint: server=Jetty(9.2.11.v20150529), title='Struts2 Showcase - Fileupload sample'

findings.conclusion: 目前不存在‘已被工具与证据共同支撑、可立即执行的安全验证 profile 路径’。因此不能声称存在特定漏洞，也不能在无 profile/前置条件的情况下继续进入漏洞确认。
evidence: 此前 vuln_profile_match 对该目标返回 []
exploit_validation 阶段仅确认 / 可达，未产生漏洞验证证据
当前无凭据、无 session、无 pivot、无额外路径发现

last_planner_decision.handoff_acceptance: Accept if the stage returns validated evidence, a supported safe-validation conclusion, or a clear determination that another agent should take over.

metadata.task_brief: Autonomously assess the strongest existing vulnerability candidate for the in-scope HTTP service on 10.20.0.10:8080 using only bounded, non-destructive methods. Use current KG evidence and available validation-oriented tools to confirm or eliminate a safe validation path, collect concrete evidence, and avoid speculative or destructive actions.

last_planner_decision.objective: Perform bounded non-destructive validation of the strongest existing candidate on 10.20.0.10:8080 and determine whether it yields concrete goal-relevant evidence or should be ruled out.
last_planner_decision.reasoning_summary: Recent analysis strengthened the 10.20.0.10:8080 fingerprint and identified it as the highest-value target. Policy and available tools allow bounded validation, while no stop condition is yet satisfied.

required_context.evidence_gap: KG and recent vuln analysis indicate a likely Struts2/Jetty web target on 10.20.0.10:8080, but there is still no validated candidate, no goal proof, and no success_condition_progress showing stop eligibility.
required_context.known_service_focus: HTTP service on 10.20.0.10:8080 with prior fingerprinting suggesting Jetty 9.2.11 and Struts2 Showcase fileupload sample.

success_criteria: A supported bounded validation profile is matched and safely checked against the 10.20.0.10:8080 target, producing concrete evidence.
Or the target is ruled out for safe bounded validation with clear justification and evidence for replanning.
Any resulting evidence is sufficient to guide either GoalAgent assessment or a different next-stage decision.

operation_input.goal: autonomously complete the authorized full_chain_lab multi-host penetration chain under supplied policy

tools.description: Run one shell command in the isolated lab environment.
tools.name: run_command

tools.description: Run an nmap service discovery scan against one or more lab targets.
tools.name: nmap_scan

tools.description: Probe one HTTP endpoint and return status, headers and a short body excerpt.
tools.name: http_probe

tools.description: Fetch a web page and extract title, server header and content type.
tools.name: web_fingerprint

tools.description: Probe common paths on a lab web target.
tools.name: web_discover

tools.description: Resolve hostnames in the lab environment.
tools.name: dns_lookup

tools.description: Inspect a TLS endpoint certificate in the lab environment.
tools.name: tls_probe

tools.description: Check whether one TCP host:port is reachable from the lab environment.
tools.name: tcp_connect_probe

tools.description: Validate one provided HTTP Basic credential against one endpoint; no brute forcing.
tools.name: http_basic_auth_check

tools.description: Match known bounded vulnerability validation profiles to observed lab service metadata.
tools.name: vuln_profile_match

tools.description: Check non-destructive preconditions for a safe validation profile against a lab target.
tools.name: validation_precheck

tools.description: Run one bounded non-destructive vulnerability validation profile against an authorized lab target.
tools.name: safe_vuln_validate

tools.description: Validate one provided credential against one lab auth service; no brute forcing.
tools.name: credential_check

tools.description: Check whether a lab session handle has enough metadata to be reused.
tools.name: session_probe

tools.description: Register a bounded lab session handle for runtime coordination.
tools.name: session_open_lab

tools.description: Return declared lab identity context facts for a session or host.
tools.name: identity_context_probe

tools.description: Return declared lab privilege context facts without running escalation payloads.
tools.name: privilege_context_probe

tools.description: Verify a candidate lab pivot route with bounded TCP or HTTP reachability checks.
tools.name: pivot_route_probe

tools.description: Probe one internal lab service using bounded TCP or HTTP checks, optionally through a registered pivot route.
tools.name: internal_service_discover

tools.description: Run bounded service discovery from a configured pivot route against an authorized restricted-zone target or CIDR.
tools.name: pivoted_nmap_scan

tools.description: Alias for bounded goal condition checks used at the end of an attack-chain validation.
tools.name: chain_goal_check

tools.description: Check a bounded goal condition such as HTTP status or body substring.
tools.name: goal_check

tools.description: Store bounded evidence text or JSON as a local artifact and return an artifact reference.
tools.name: artifact_store

tools.description: Execute a bounded, lab-authorized exploit via a pre-registered exploit profile. exploit_profile_id must reference a profile in configs/exploit_profiles/. Only operations declared in the profile's allowed_operations are executed. All evidence is written to /opt/aegra/ paths. Raw markers are never returned.
tools.name: lab_authorized_exploit_execute

tools.description: Observe post-access lab artifacts from declared drop zones (/opt/aegra/flags/, /opt/aegra/hints/, /opt/aegra/loot/). Returns file names and types only. Flag contents are returned as SHA-256 hashes. Never reads /etc/, /root/, or any system paths.
tools.name: post_access_observe

tools.description: Read a specific lab goal marker and return its HMAC hash for proof submission. The raw marker value is never returned. Use the returned submission_hash as proof_token in GoalAgent evidence.
tools.name: read_lab_marker

tools.description: Register a validated pivot route for runtime coordination. Requires destination_host and destination_port to already be confirmed reachable via pivot_route_probe. Returns a route_id for use by internal tools.
tools.name: pivot_route_register

tools.description: Check a goal condition on an internal service reachable via a registered pivot route. Requires route_id or session_id. Returns goal_satisfied and opaque evidence_refs. Never returns raw marker or token values.
tools.name: internal_goal_check

tools.description: Read a bounded, non-destructive proof from an authorized data service reachable through existing access or a pivot route. Connection secrets are resolved from lab configuration/environment and raw values are not returned.
tools.name: controlled_data_read_proof

tools.description: Return a redacted summary of the current success_condition_progress from runtime metadata. Reports eligible_for_stop, satisfied conditions, and missing conditions. Never exposes private rubric or raw proof values.
tools.name: success_condition_check
